name: Triptans

dosing: Sumatriptan 6mg subcutaneous injection is the gold standard acute treatment, aborting most attacks within 10-15 minutes
Zolmitriptan 5mg nasal spray is the preferred non-injection option, effective within 15-20 minutes
Oral triptans (sumatriptan 50-100mg, rizatriptan 10mg) are widely considered too slow for cluster attacks but used when nothing else is available
Many in the community limit triptan use strictly due to rebound concerns and interference with psychedelic busting
preparations: Subcutaneous auto-injector pen is the most common delivery method, though some split doses using vials and syringes to stretch supply
Nasal spray should be inhaled gently through the nostril on the affected side
Oral tablets and dissolving wafers are a last resort when injections and spray are unavailable
schedule: Inject at the very first sign of an attack for fastest results; delays reduce effectiveness significantly
Strictly limit frequency of use to avoid medication overuse headaches and cycle prolongation
A 5+ day triptan washout is required before attempting psychedelic busting, as triptans block serotonin receptor activity

sideEffects: Rebound headaches and cycle prolongation with frequent use
Loss of effectiveness over time with repeated use
Chest tightness, pressure, or breathing difficulty
Dizziness and feeling loopy or zombie-like
Painful detox period when stopping before busting
High cost and insurance supply limitations
